findings.detail: Seven nodes (S1, S2, S3 in LIST STRUCTURE subgraph; L1, L2, L3, L4 in FOR-LOOP TRAVERSAL subgraph) in two direction-TB subgraphs under a flowchart LR root. All bold labels are 2‚Äì3 words. Every label traces directly to corpus ¬ß5/¬ß6 Core Concepts: 'List Variable' (¬ß5.1 'A list is a Python value ‚Ä¶ under a single variable name'), 'Items by Index' (¬ß5.2 index table [0]=72 ‚Ä¶ [4]=55), 'append() and len()' (¬ß5.4 len(), ¬ß5.5 append()), 'Start Iteration' (¬ß5.6 'for mark in marks:'), 'Current Item' (¬ß5.6 'the variable item automatically holds the next value'), 'Process Item' (¬ß5.6 'code that runs once for each item' / ¬ß6 Step 3 iterate to compute total), 'All Items Visited' (¬ß5.6 'When the loop finishes'). No downstream or out-of-scope concepts (list comprehensions, slicing, sorting, dictionaries) present. Title 'Python List ‚Äî Structure and For-Loop Traversal' sits in the config title: field ‚Äî correct per title-discipline rule. Each subgraph is a clean single-path linear flow (S1‚ÜíS2‚ÜíS3; L1‚ÜíL2‚ÜíL3‚ÜíL4); no branching within either panel. STRUCT‚ÜíLOOP cross-subgraph edge labelled 'iterate over' makes the relationship between storage structure and traversal explicit and beginner-readable. classDef tiers: start (#a5d8ff blue), act (#a5d8ff blue), auto (#d0bfff purple), done (#b2f2bb green) ‚Äî all match SKILL.md classDef definitions. Subgraph backgrounds differentiated: STRUCT blue-tinted (#f0f4ff / blue stroke) for storage panel, LOOP green-tinted (#f4fff0 / green stroke) for traversal panel.

Side-by-side panel layout (LR root + direction-TB subgraphs) exactly matches the diagram intent: 'Ordered list of boxes showing the list structure, followed by an iteration flow arrow stepping through each box in sequence ‚Äî illustrates both the storage structure and the for-loop traversal in one visual'. Left panel captures list structure (variable ‚Üí indexed items ‚Üí operations); right panel captures iteration flow (start ‚Üí current item ‚Üí process ‚Üí done). Follows the canonical 12-1 / 12-3 golden-dataset side-by-side pattern.

All 7 nodes map exclusively to ¬ß5.1 (List Variable), ¬ß5.2 (Items by Index), ¬ß5.4‚Äì¬ß5.5 (append/len), ¬ß5.6 (for-loop iteration phases). The subtitle text in S2 ‚Äî '[0]=72 [1]=88 [2]=65 [3]=90 [4]=55' ‚Äî directly reproduces the corpus ¬ß5.2 index table, making the zero-based indexing concrete for beginners. No advanced Python constructs present.
